name: Monestery Occupation 1975
column4: 1975:  AIM occupies an abandoned monastery near Gresham
column5: Wisconsin.  The stand-off ends 34 days later.

name: Fort Robinson Protest - November 1972
description: November 1972:  50 Indians occupy the Nebraska Historical Society Museum at Fort Robinson State Park.  Led by Bob Yellow Bird (Steele)
column4: a young Oglala student at Chadron State College.  Occupation ends after 14 hours.

name: Wounded Knee Protest
column4: February 27
column5: 1973:  AIM members and supporters invaded Wounded Knee
column7: and establish fortifications.  The FBI and BIA establish roadblocks around the perimeter.  The occupation lasts seventy-one days.

name: Custer Riot
column4: 1973:  AIM members arrive in Custer to make their complaints known with town officials over the Wesley Bad Heart Bull death a week earlier.  A riot ensues; rocks and bottles are thrown and a small building is burned to the ground.

name: BIA Protest 1972
column4: 1972:  AIM members arrive in Washington
column5: D.C. in the Trail of Broken Treaties.  AIM storms the BIA headquarters after a government snafu is interpreted as a double-cross.  Protesters hold the building for six days
column6: leaving after government officials agree to pay $67
column7: 000 in travel money to leave the city.  AIM vandalizes the building and steals several thousand documents.

name: Boy Scouts Protest
column4: 1972:  AIM members and sympathizers demonstrate against an Indian dance being performed by Boy Scouts in Topeka
column5: Kansas.  A fist fight breaks out and five AIM members are arrested.

name: Wounded Knee Protest 1972
column4: 1972:  Russell Means leads 300 Indians to Wounded Knee after Raymond Yellow Thunder's funeral.  The Trading Post is looted and $50

name: Fort Totten Protest
column4: 1972:  AIM seizes a BIA jail in Fort Totten

name: Gordon Protest
column4: 1972:  AIM leads a protest in Gordon
column6: after the death of Raymond Yellow Thunder.

name: BIA Protest 1971
column4: 1971:  Russell Means leads 60 protesters against the Bureau of Indian Affairs building in Washington

name: AIM Sit-In
description: June 1971:  AIM stages a sit-in at an abandoned U.S. Army Nike Missile Site near Chicago
column4: Illinois.

name: Mount Rushmore Protest
column4: 1971:  Russell Means leads 50 Indians in a demonstration at Mount Rushmore

name: Conference on Indian Affairs
column4: 1970:  Three-day conference on Indian affairs at the Airlie Center in Warrenton
column5: Virginia.  Intoxicated AIM members cause several thousand dollars worth of damage.

name: Augustana Protest
description: July 1970:  Dennis Banks leads AIM members in a protest against a Lutheran church in Sioux Falls
column4: SD.  AIM occupies Bergsaker Dormitory.

name: AIM Sit-In
column4: 1971:  AIM stages a sit-in at Fort Snelling

column4: AIM members and supporters arrive in Rapid City

name: Coast Guard Station Takeover
column4: 1971:  Herb Powless leads the takeover of a Coast Guard station.

name: Department of Public Instruction Takeover
column4: 1973:  Ron Petite leads the occupation of the State Department of Public Instruction building in Des Moines in an attempt to call attention to discrimination allegations.

name: Pembroke Protest
description: Vernon Bellecourt and 50 other Indians affiliated with AIM are arrested for trying to use a former all-Indian school to hold a meeting.
